name: Acheron River
description: The Acheron River, also known as the River of Woe, holds a prominent and chilling place within Greek mythology. It is one of the five rivers of the Underworld, serving as a boundary between the world of the living and the realm of the dead ruled by Hades. The Acheron is not merely a geographical feature but a symbolic representation of sorrow, pain, and the finality of death. Souls of the deceased were ferried across its murky, stagnant waters by Charon, the grim ferryman, who demanded payment for his services, typically a coin placed under the tongue of the deceased before burial. Those without the proper payment were doomed to wander the banks of the Acheron for a hundred years, unable to find rest. The river's dark and foreboding nature reflected the despair and hopelessness associated with death, making it a powerful and evocative symbol in Greek literature and art. The very name 'Acheron' is derived from the Greek word 'akhos,' meaning distress or grief, further emphasizing its connection to suffering and mourning. Its presence in the Underworld underscores the Greeks' complex relationship with death and the afterlife.
subtitle: River of Woe and Passage to Hades

keyMyths.title: Charon's Ferry
keyMyths.description: The myth of Charon details his role in ferrying souls across the Acheron, emphasizing the need for proper burial rites to ensure passage to the afterlife. Souls without payment are condemned to wander the banks.

keyMyths.title: Heracles and the Underworld
keyMyths.description: Heracles journeyed to the Underworld as one of his twelve labors and likely crossed the Acheron, showcasing the river's role as a gateway for both the living and the dead. His journey highlights the river’s accessibility by heroes.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Provides early descriptions of the Underworld and its rivers.
sources.text: Provides early descriptions of the Underworld and its rivers.

sources.author: Virgil
sources.description: Details Aeneas' journey to the Underworld and his encounter with Charon and the Acheron.
sources.text: Details Aeneas' journey to the Underworld and his encounter with Charon and the Acheron.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Provides background on the origins of the gods and their roles, including those associated with the Underworld.
